summary.intent: Address a second round of Codex automated review findings on PR #669 (ars-ui combobox component), fixing six identified behavioral bugs and syncing the spec documentation.
summary.outcome: All six Codex review threads were resolved with a follow-up commit (fixing multi-select custom commits/reverts, controlled input sync, disabled UpdateItems gating, Set::All hidden-input serialization, and listbox label fallback), CI passed, and a third Codex review pass was triggered.

repo: The repo uses a `cargo xci --profile fast` gate as the pre-push validation pipeline, running fmt, check, clippy, nextest (5,900+ tests), integration, and adapter tests in sequence.
Codex review state is tracked via GitHub reaction emojis on the PR: `eyes` means review in progress, `+1` means approval, absence of both after `eyes` drops means findings were posted.
Snapshot tests use `cargo insta` with `--unreferenced=reject` enforcement; accepted snapshots must be committed alongside implementation changes and tracked with a `snapshot-reviewed` label.
The project has a `cargo xtask spec validate` command for validating spec markdown files, and `cargo xtask spec toc` for generating table of contents.
The combobox spec lives at `spec/components/selection/combobox.md` and must be kept in sync with implementation contract changes.
GraphQL mutations via `gh api graphql` are used to resolve PR review threads programmatically using thread IDs.

code.finding: Blur handling must route through close_plan and emit on_open_change(false) rather than directly closing state.
Enter with no highlighted item must dispatch CommitInput and branch on allow_custom_value to either commit or revert raw input.
UpdateItems must recompute visible keys from the replacement collection and current input value, not stale state.
Multi-select custom value commits must preserve selection mode and clear input after commit; single-select path was incorrectly reused.
Set::All hidden-input serialization used a colliding key; fixed to use __ars_all sentinel to avoid conflicts with item values.
Listbox aria-label fallback: content_attrs must fall back to component label/aria-labelledby when no explicit listbox label is provided.

workflow: Test-first discipline: regression tests were written and confirmed failing before applying implementation fixes, providing clear proof the tests exercised the actual bugs.
Codex review is polled via a combination of reaction emoji state and unresolved thread count using GraphQL queries, with 120-second sleep intervals between polls.
After a `.snap` file changes, the `snapshot-reviewed` PR label must be re-applied even if it was already present from a prior commit.
Coverage reporting uses `cargo llvm-cov` with `--show-missing-lines` to identify uncovered lines; combobox achieved ~97% region coverage after fixes.
Reply-and-resolve on Codex threads is done atomically: POST a reply referencing the fix commit SHA, then immediately call the resolveReviewThread GraphQL mutation.

friction: Multiple 120-second polling sleep intervals were needed to wait for Codex's async review completion, adding ~10+ minutes of idle waiting time per review cycle.
The Codex review cycle required a third trigger after the second fix batch, meaning the workflow requires multiple full iterations of fix→push→wait→review.
CI checks (Clippy, Mutual Exclusion Guard, Unit Tests) ran sequentially and took significant time, requiring additional polling to track their completion.
The `cargo xci --profile fast` gate compiles and runs 5,900+ tests, making iteration cycles slow even on the 'fast' profile.
open_items: Unit Tests CI check was still pending when the session ended; final CI green status was not confirmed in the transcript.
A third `@codex review` pass was triggered but no findings or approval from it were captured in this session.
The combobox spec sync covered the six Codex-identified issues but may need a broader audit pass for other contract sections not touched in this session.
The `__ars_all` sentinel value for Set::All hidden-input serialization should be documented in the spec's hidden input section to prevent future misunderstanding.
